findings.evidence: `rg -n "@\[simp\]|mem_completeLinearSystem_iff_eq_zero" code/TauCeti/AlgebraicGeometry/WeilDivisor/LinearSystem.lean` shows only `@[simp] lemma mem_completeLinearSystem` at line 59; the new characteristic iff lemmas at lines 166, 183, 280, and 289 have no automation annotation.
findings.fix: Mark the chosen normal-form degree-zero membership iff lemmas with `@[simp]` for both weighted and unweighted variants, e.g. the divisor-class versions at lines 166 and 280, or one consistent equivalent normal form.
findings.issue: The new degree-zero membership characterizations are missing `@[simp]`, so `simp` on `E ∈ S.completeLinearSystem D` under `weightedDegree w D = 0` or `degree D = 0` keeps using the generic `mem_completeLinearSystem` normal form instead of reducing membership to `E = 0` plus the zero-class/equivalence condition.

summary: The characteristic degree-zero membership API is present, but it is not registered as the normal-form simplification, so users still get the generic effective-and-equivalent shape from `simp` instead of the new rigidity characterization.
